subtasks.description: Query Salesforce licensing data to retrieve licensed seat count and actual seat assignments by user type and organizational unit, then identify unutilized licenses by comparing provisioned vs. actively assigned seats.

Reconcile Salesforce licensed seat count with actual seat assignments to identify gaps, classify utilization posture (high/medium/low), and generate recommendations for license optimization and cost reduction.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks required: (1) sql_gen at mid tier to fetch and compare licensed vs. assigned seats from what are likely multiple Salesforce licensing and user assignment tables with aggregation by dimension; (2) cross_recon at mid tier to reconcile the two datasets, classify utilization patterns, and recommend actions. Both mid tier justified—not simple single-table queries, but not requiring frontier-level complexity. No separate policy_action needed since compliance and governance evaluation (if any) is part of the reconciliation logic. No multitool_plan required—this is a standard data analytics flow within Salesforce data warehouse.

output.sql: SELECT publisher, product, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_active_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS distinct_users, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) AS licensed_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) - COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unutilized_licenses FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Salesforce' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY publisher, product ORDER BY unutilized_licenses DESC
